description: Monitoring scan of the live Olist DataSource (no anomalies pre-filtered). 'Empty' is a slight misnomer per the seeded data: this captures the agent's typical 'I see no significant change' / 'I see one or two ambient findings' shape, which is the boring-case baseline. The real seeded-3 anomaly case is fixture 02.

input.notes: MonitoringAgent.scan() takes no fixture-tunable arg beyond the runnable-categories list. We pass [] (broad scan) so the agent does an unconstrained Olist-fallback pass, mirroring how the briefing route behaves when the schema doesn't fit any of the 10 hardcoded categories.

golden_output.impact: Voucher payment value has collapsed from a R$ 1,553/week baseline to R$ 320/week in the most recent 4 weeks — an 80% wipe-out suggesting the voucher programme has effectively ceased or been revoked, stranding any customers who relied on it.

Debit card payment value has slipped from a R$ 10,128/week baseline to R$ 8,343/week in the last 4 weeks — a consistent downward staircase (R$ 11,932 → R$ 6,284) suggesting customers are migrating away from debit to boleto or credit_card, which may raise chargeback or instalment cost exposure.
São Paulo revenue is holding flat at approximately R$ 49,971/week vs a R$ 50,267/week baseline — no meaningful change detected; the largest state is not contributing to any national topline pressure.

scoring_config.notes: Monitoring output is an array of Anomaly objects. We don't require any specific fields because the empty/ambient case can be []. The similarity judge does the heavy lifting: 'did the new run reach the same anomaly set as the golden?'
